changes.description: Rewrote skill to produce static inline SVG instead of D3.js dynamic DOM generation. Key changes: (1) Eliminated D3.js library dependency entirely — no download/vendor step needed, (2) Instructed model to write literal <svg>, <rect>, <path> tags directly in HTML source instead of generating them via JavaScript, (3) Added explicit coordinate computation formulas for bar and line charts, (4) Added critical rules: use relative file paths, no CDN links, output to chart.html, (5) Provided complete working examples with pre-computed SVG elements, (6) Removed all D3 API code (scales, axes, selections) and replaced with manual SVG math

root_cause_analysis.bar-chart_failures: Tests check for literal '<svg' and '<rect' strings in HTML source. D3's .append('svg') and .join('rect') generate elements via JavaScript at runtime, so these tags don't exist in the static HTML file.
root_cause_analysis.line-chart_failures: Model used absolute file path (/workspace/timeseries.csv) instead of relative path, wasted time downloading D3 via curl, and timed out before completing the write operation.
